---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
library(dplyr)

# ---------------------------
# Helper functions
# ---------------------------

bin_to_str <- function(v) paste(v, collapse="")

# Generate all states
states <- lapply(0:7, function(i) as.integer(intToBits(i))[1:3])
state_labels <- sapply(states, bin_to_str)

# ---------------------------
# NEW Boolean networks
# ---------------------------

# Model 1: rotation (no self-loop)
net1 <- function(x) {
  c(
    x[3],
    x[1],
    x[2]
  )
}

# Model 2: self-loop on x2
net2 <- function(x) {
  c(
    x[3],
    x[1] | x[2],
    x[2]
  )
}

# ---------------------------
# Canonical cycle representation
# (important for clean heatmap)
# ---------------------------

canonical_cycle <- function(cycle_states) {
  strs <- apply(cycle_states, 1, bin_to_str)
  
  # remove duplicate last state if present
  if (length(strs) > 1 && strs[1] == strs[length(strs)]) {
    strs <- strs[-length(strs)]
  }
  
  # generate all rotations
  rotations <- sapply(seq_along(strs), function(i) {
    paste(c(strs[i:length(strs)], strs[1:(i-1)]), collapse="→")
  })
  
  # pick lexicographically smallest string
  return(min(rotations))
}

# ---------------------------
# Simulation until attractor
# ---------------------------

simulate_to_attractor <- function(net, init, max_steps=20) {
  history <- matrix(NA, nrow=max_steps, ncol=3)
  history[1,] <- init
  
  for (t in 2:max_steps) {
    history[t,] <- net(history[t-1,])
    
    # FIXED POINT
    if (all(history[t,] == history[t-1,])) {
      return(bin_to_str(history[t,]))
    }
    
    # CYCLE DETECTION
    for (i in 1:(t-1)) {
      if (all(history[t,] == history[i,])) {
        cycle <- history[i:(t-1), , drop=FALSE]
        return(canonical_cycle(cycle))
      }
    }
  }
  
  return("NA")
}

# ---------------------------
# Run models
# ---------------------------

run_model <- function(net, model_name) {
  data.frame(
    init = state_labels,
    model = model_name,
    attractor = sapply(states, function(s) simulate_to_attractor(net, s))
  )
}

df1 <- run_model(net1, "No self-loop")
df2 <- run_model(net2, "With self-loop")

df <- rbind(df1, df2)

# ---------------------------
# Factor ordering
# ---------------------------

df$init <- factor(df$init, levels = state_labels)
df$model <- factor(df$model, levels = c("No self-loop", "With self-loop"))

#write.csv(df, "attractorsToy.csv", row.names = FALSE)
df <- read.csv("attractorsToy.csv", colClasses = "character")
# ---------------------------
# Plot heatmap
# ---------------------------

df$attractor <- sapply(df$attractor, clean_cycle)
attr <- ggplot(df, aes(x=model, y=init, fill=attractor)) +
  geom_tile(color="white", linewidth=0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label=attractor), size=4) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette="Set3") +
  labs(
    title="Attractor Mapping: Effect of Self-loop",
    x="Model",
    y="Initial State"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle=20, hjust=1)
  )

attr
```

```{r}
library(igraph)

# ---------------------------
# helper: detect attractor nodes
# ---------------------------

is_attractor <- function(edges) {
  tab <- table(edges$from, edges$to)
  diag_vals <- diag(tab)
  names(diag_vals) <- rownames(tab)
  diag_vals > 0
}

# ---------------------------
# recompute graphs
# ---------------------------

g1 <- graph_from_data_frame(edges1, directed=TRUE, vertices=basin1)
g2 <- graph_from_data_frame(edges2, directed=TRUE, vertices=basin2)

# ---------------------------
# colors (basins)
# ---------------------------

V(g1)$color <- get_colors(basin1)
V(g2)$color <- get_colors(basin2)

# ---------------------------
# highlight attractors (fixed points)
# ---------------------------

V(g1)$size <- ifelse(V(g1)$name %in% c("000","111"), 45, 30)
V(g2)$size <- ifelse(V(g2)$name %in% c("000","111"), 45, 30)

# ---------------------------
# edge styling: emphasize self-loops
# ---------------------------

E(g1)$width <- ifelse(get.edgelist(g1)[,1] == get.edgelist(g1)[,2], 2, 0.7)
E(g2)$width <- ifelse(get.edgelist(g2)[,1] == get.edgelist(g2)[,2], 2, 0.7)

E(g1)$arrow.size <- 0.5
E(g2)$arrow.size <- 0.5

# ---------------------------
# layout (fixed for comparison)
# ---------------------------

layout <- layout_with_fr(g1)

# ---------------------------
# plot
# ---------------------------

par(mfrow=c(1,2), mar=c(1,1,3,1))

plot(
  g1,
  layout = layout,
  main = "Model 1: No self-loop (Basin STG)",
  vertex.label.cex = 0.9
)

plot(
  g2,
  layout = layout,
  main = "Model 2: With self-loop (Basin STG)",
  vertex.label.cex = 0.9
)

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(igraph)
library(RColorBrewer)

# ---------------------------
# Boolean models
# ---------------------------

net1 <- function(x) c(x[3], x[1], x[2])                 # rotation
net2 <- function(x) c(x[3], x[1] | x[2], x[2])          # self-loop

# ---------------------------
# state space
# ---------------------------

states <- lapply(0:7, function(i) as.integer(intToBits(i))[1:3])
state_names <- sapply(states, paste0, collapse="")

# ---------------------------
# attractor simulation
# ---------------------------

simulate_to_attractor <- function(net, init, max_steps=20) {
  hist <- matrix(NA, nrow=max_steps, ncol=3)
  hist[1,] <- init
  
  for (t in 2:max_steps) {
    hist[t,] <- net(hist[t-1,])
    
    # fixed point
    if (all(hist[t,] == hist[t-1,])) {
      return(paste(hist[t,], collapse=""))
    }
    
    # cycle detection
    for (i in 1:(t-1)) {
      if (all(hist[t,] == hist[i,])) {
        cyc <- hist[i:(t-1), , drop=FALSE]
        return(paste(apply(cyc,1,paste0,collapse=""), collapse="→"))
      }
    }
  }
  "NA"
}

# ---------------------------
# basin computation
# ---------------------------

get_basin <- function(net) {
  data.frame(
    state = state_names,
    basin = sapply(states, function(s) simulate_to_attractor(net, s))
  )
}

basin1 <- get_basin(net1)
basin2 <- get_basin(net2)

# ---------------------------
# CONSISTENT attractor palette (Set3 like ggplot)
# ---------------------------

all_attr <- sort(unique(c(basin1$basin, basin2$basin)))
cols <- brewer.pal(max(3, length(all_attr)), "Set3")[1:length(all_attr)]
attr_col <- setNames(cols, all_attr)

# ---------------------------
# edge builder
# ---------------------------

build_edges <- function(net) {
  do.call(rbind, lapply(states, function(s) {
    data.frame(
      from = paste(s, collapse=""),
      to   = paste(net(s), collapse="")
    )
  }))
}

edges1 <- build_edges(net1)
edges2 <- build_edges(net2)

# ---------------------------
# graphs
# ---------------------------

g1 <- graph_from_data_frame(edges1, directed=TRUE)
g2 <- graph_from_data_frame(edges2, directed=TRUE)

V(g1)$basin <- basin1$basin
V(g2)$basin <- basin2$basin

V(g1)$color <- attr_col[V(g1)$basin]
V(g2)$color <- attr_col[V(g2)$basin]

V(g1)$size <- ifelse(V(g1)$name %in% c("000","111"), 45, 30)
V(g2)$size <- ifelse(V(g2)$name %in% c("000","111"), 45, 30)

# ---------------------------
# layout (shared)
# ---------------------------

layout <- layout_with_fr(g1)

# ---------------------------
# HEATMAP (same colors)
# ---------------------------

df <- read.csv("attractorsToy.csv", colClasses = "character")

p <- ggplot(df, aes(x=model, y=init, fill=attractor)) +
  geom_tile(color="white") +
  geom_text(aes(label=attractor), size=3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=attr_col) +
  labs(title="Attractor Mapping (Consistent with STG)", x="Model", y="State") +
  theme_minimal()

print(p)

# ---------------------------
# STG (same colors)
# ---------------------------

par(mfrow=c(1,2), mar=c(1,1,3,1))

plot(g1, layout=layout, main="Model 1: No self-loop",
     vertex.label.cex=0.9, edge.arrow.size=0.5)

plot(g2, layout=layout, main="Model 2: With self-loop",
     vertex.label.cex=0.9, edge.arrow.size=0.5)
```
